content.problemStatement:
Chef had a sequence S0,S1,…,SN−1S_0, S_1, \ldots, S_{N-1}S0​,S1​,…,SN−1​; each element of this sequence was an integer between 000 and N−1N-1N−1 (inclusive). Unfortunately, he forgot some (possibly zero or all) elements of this sequence. You are given a sequence A0,A1,…,AN−1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0​,A1​,…,AN−1​, where for each valid iii, Ai=−1A_i = -1Ai​=−1 denotes an element Chef forgot and if Ai≠−1A_i \neq -1Ai​=−1, then Ai=SiA_i = S_iAi​=Si​.

Before Chef forgot any elements of SSS, he created a sequence B0,B1,…,BN−1B_0, B_1, \ldots, B_{N-1}B0​,B1​,…,BN−1​, where for each valid iii, BiB_iBi​ is the number of occurrences of the value iii in SSS (the number of valid indices jjj such that Sj=iS_j = iSj​=i), and then, he created a third sequence G0,G1,…,GNG_0, G_1, \ldots, G_NG0​,G1​,…,GN​, where for each valid iii, GiG_iGi​ is the number of occurrences of the value iii in BBB. (Note that the elements of BBB are between 000 and NNN inclusive.) Unfortunately, Chef also forgot the sequence BBB, but he remembers GGG.

Help Chef restore the missing elements of the sequence SSS. Precisely, find the lexicographically smallest sequence SSS which is consistent with all the given information or determine that there is no such sequence.
constraints: −1≤Ai≤N−1-1 \le A_i \le N-1−1≤Ai​≤N−1 for each valid iii
0≤Gi≤N0 \le G_i \le N0≤Gi​≤N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500500500
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A0,A1,…,AN−1A_0, A_1, \ldots, A_{N-1}A0​,A1​,…,AN−1​.
The third line contains N+1N+1N+1 space-separated integers G0,G1,…,GNG_0, G_1, \ldots, G_NG0​,G1​,…,GN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no sequence SSS consistent with all the given information, print a single line containing the string `"impossible"`.
Otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers S0,S1,…,SN−1S_0, S_1, \ldots, S_{N-1}S0​,S1​,…,SN−1​ - the lexicographically smallest valid sequence SSS.
